I wish I had studied more back then.

I should have said yes.

Sandra, is there one decision from your past you sometimes look back on?
Yes. I should have kept studying English when I was young. I wish I had practiced more.
I understand. But with hindsight, was that really a turning point?
Maybe. When I reflect, that path led me right here. So perhaps I am better off than I thought.
That is a lovely way to see it. So what is the lesson you learned?
That it is never too late. The outcome is that I am here now, so I forgive myself and move on.
I should have
I wish I had
When I reflect,
With hindsight, that was a turning point.
I take the lesson, and I move on.

Let me look back at one decision for a moment. Years ago, I was offered the chance to live and work abroad for two years. I said no. I was comfortable, and I did not want to move. Looking back now, I think I should have said yes. I should have taken the risk. I wish I had been a little braver back then. That was a real turning point, and I chose the safe path. For a long time I felt sorry about it. But here is the thing about hindsight: it is always perfect. It is easy to know the right answer after the moment has passed. So now, instead of blaming myself, I try to learn the lesson and move on. I am better off thinking about it calmly. One outcome of that old choice is that I value courage more today. So even a regret can teach you something good.

I want to reflect on something I look back on. When I was younger, I did not keep studying English. I stopped after a few years because I felt too busy. Now I sometimes think I should have continued. I wish I had practiced more when I was young. If only I had not given up so easily. For a while, that felt like a heavy regret. But when I reflect honestly, I see that every path teaches you something. That old decision was a turning point, and it led me, in the end, right here, to this class. So maybe I am better off than I think. The lesson I learned is simple: it is never too late to begin again. I cannot change the past, so I forgive myself, take the good from it, and move on. The best outcome is that today I am here, learning, and I am proud of that.

First, think of a past decision you look back on, like a job you did not take. Then, to talk about the better choice, say should have and a past participle, for example I should have said yes. Next, to express the regret as a wish, use I wish I had and a past participle, like I wish I had been braver. After that, remember that hindsight is always perfect, so be gentle with yourself. Finally, take the lesson, forgive yourself, and move on.
